title: Formation as The Pale Ones
description: Anders Colsefni, Shawn Crahan, and Paul Gray form The Pale Ones in Des Moines, Iowa. Joey Jordison and Josh Brainard soon join, and the band plays their first show on November 5, 1995, under the name Meld.

title: Renamed to Slipknot
description: Joey Jordison suggests renaming the band to Slipknot, after one of their own songs, during a late-night session at the Sinclair gas station where he worked nights. The band begins recording at SR Audio in Des Moines.

title: Mate. Feed. Kill. Repeat. Released
description: Slipknot self-releases their debut demo album 'Mate. Feed. Kill. Repeat.' on Halloween, self-financing the approximately $40,000 production cost. The album receives limited local airplay but fails to attract major label interest.

title: Corey Taylor Joins as Vocalist
description: Corey Taylor is recruited from fellow Des Moines band Stone Sour to provide more melodic vocals. Original vocalist Anders Colsefni moves to backing vocals before ultimately leaving the band in September 1997.

title: Sid Wilson Joins; Lineup Becomes Nine
description: DJ Sid Wilson is recruited as Slipknot's ninth and final member, completing the classic nine-piece lineup. Producer Ross Robinson offers to produce their debut album after attending rehearsals.

title: Signed to Roadrunner Records
description: Slipknot signs a $500,000, seven-album deal with Roadrunner Records, marking their entry into the major label world. The signing comes after a bidding war sparked by their intense live shows and growing underground buzz.

title: Jim Root Replaces Josh Brainard
description: Guitarist Josh Brainard leaves the band during the recording of their debut album, citing dissatisfaction with certain decisions. Jim Root is recruited to complete the lineup and finish the album sessions in Malibu, California.

title: Self-Titled Debut Album Released
description: Slipknot releases their self-titled debut album, which peaks at #51 on the Billboard 200 and is eventually certified 3x Platinum by the RIAA. The album's aggressive sound and the band's shocking image launch them into mainstream consciousness.

title: Ozzfest Breakthrough
description: Slipknot plays Ozzfest 1999, dramatically expanding their audience and establishing them as one of the most exciting live acts in heavy metal. Their incendiary performances become the stuff of legend among festival attendees.

title: Iowa Released
description: Slipknot's second album 'Iowa' is released, peaking at #3 on the Billboard 200 and #1 in the UK. Darker, heavier, and more extreme than its predecessor, Iowa is widely considered the band's most brutal and uncompromising work.

title: First Hiatus and Side Projects
description: Internal conflicts force Slipknot into their first hiatus. Members pursue side projects: Taylor and Root revive Stone Sour, Jordison creates Murderdolls, and Crahan forms To My Surprise. The future of Slipknot is uncertain.

title: Vol. 3: (The Subliminal Verses) Released
description: Produced by Rick Rubin, Slipknot's third album marks a creative evolution with more melodic and experimental elements while maintaining their heaviness. It peaks at #2 on the Billboard 200 and produces the hit single 'Duality'.

title: First Grammy Award Win
description: Slipknot wins their first Grammy Award for Best Metal Performance for the single 'Before I Forget' at the 48th Annual Grammy Awards, cementing their status as one of the premier metal bands of their generation.

title: All Hope Is Gone Released
description: Slipknot's fourth album 'All Hope Is Gone' becomes their first to debut at #1 on the Billboard 200. It also tops charts in multiple countries and produces singles including 'Psychosocial' and the emotional ballad 'Snuff'.

title: Death of Paul Gray
description: Founding bassist Paul Gray is found dead in an Urbandale, Iowa, hotel room at age 38. His death is later confirmed as an accidental overdose of morphine and fentanyl. The remaining band members hold an emotional unmasked press conference the following day.

title: First Knotfest Festival
description: Slipknot launches their own music festival, Knotfest, at Mid-America Motorplex near Pacific Junction, Iowa. Featuring bands like Deftones, Lamb of God, and Serj Tankian, the festival includes a Slipknot museum and becomes an annual tradition.

title: Joey Jordison Dismissed from the Band
description: Co-founding drummer Joey Jordison is dismissed from Slipknot after 18 years. He later reveals he was suffering from transverse myelitis, a neurological disease that affected his ability to play. Jordison stated he 'did not quit' and was 'blindsided' by the news.

title: .5: The Gray Chapter Released
description: Slipknot's fifth album, dedicated to the late Paul Gray, debuts at #1 on the Billboard 200. New members Alessandro Venturella (bass) and Jay Weinberg (drums) join the lineup. The album is described as 'very dark' and a cross between Iowa and Vol. 3.

title: Chris Fehn Dismissed
description: Percussionist Chris Fehn is dismissed from Slipknot after filing a lawsuit against the band over alleged financial mismanagement and withheld payments. He is replaced by Michael Pfaff for touring and future recordings.

title: We Are Not Your Kind Released
description: Slipknot's sixth album becomes their third consecutive #1 on the Billboard 200 and their first UK #1 in 18 years since Iowa. The album is praised for its experimental approach while maintaining the band's signature intensity.

title: Death of Joey Jordison
description: Former founding drummer Joey Jordison dies peacefully in his sleep at age 46. His family attributes his death to an unspecified chronic condition. The band and metal community pay tribute to one of the genre's greatest drummers.

title: The End, So Far Released
description: Slipknot releases their seventh studio album and final record under their Roadrunner Records contract. The album experiments with a wider sonic palette while retaining the band's aggressive core, and debuts at #2 on the Billboard 200.

title: Eloy Casagrande Joins as Drummer
description: Brazilian drummer Eloy Casagrande, formerly of Sepultura, joins Slipknot as Jay Weinberg's replacement. His addition signals a new chapter as the band begins work on their eighth studio album and embarks on 25th anniversary touring.
